title: Restaurant POS Systems 2026: Top 8 Platforms for Food Service Operations
description: Compare top restaurant POS systems 2026 for table management, kitchen display, menu ordering, and payment processing. Toast, Revel, TouchBistro features and pricing.
content:
Restaurant POS Specialization in 2026

Restaurant POS systems provide specialized features for food service operations distinct from retail POS platforms. The 2026 restaurant POS market offers platforms handling table management, kitchen display systems, menu modification workflows, guest management, and delivery integration. Restaurants require POS systems designed for their unique operational complexity including timed service, kitchen coordination, and guest experience management.

Restaurant-specific POS features include table mapping, split checks, course timing, kitchen display integration, modifier handling, tip processing, and reservation management. These features distinguish restaurant POS from general retail platforms attempting food service support.

Top 8 Restaurant POS Systems Comparison

Our analysis evaluates restaurant POS platforms across food service-specific features:

PlatformMonthly CostKDS SupportTable ManagementDelivery IntegrationMenu FeaturesBest For
Toast$0-165NativeExcellentNativeExcellentAll Restaurants
Revel Systems$99-199NativeExcellentIntegrationExcellentEnterprise Chains
TouchBistro$69-399NativeExcellentIntegrationExcellentiPad Restaurants
Square for Restaurants$0-60Add-onBasicAdd-onGoodCasual Dining
Lightspeed Restaurant$69-199NativeGoodIntegrationGoodMulti-location
POSitouchCustomNativeExcellentYesExcellentFull Service
NCR AlohaCustomNativeExcellentYesExcellentLarge Chains
Breadcrumb POS$99NativeGoodIntegrationGoodBars/Pubs

Toast: Restaurant POS Leader

Toast remains the premier restaurant POS system in 2026, built specifically for food service from inception. The platform handles all restaurant types from quick-service to fine dining with industry-specific features. Toast's all-in-one approach includes POS, Kitchen Display System, online ordering, and delivery management.

  • Native Kitchen Display System (KDS) for kitchen coordination
  • Table mapping with visual layout management
  • Menu builder with complex modifier support
  • Course timing for multi-course service
  • Split checks and payment flexibility
  • Guest management and CRM features
  • Delivery platform integration (DoorDash, UberEats)
  • Online ordering with Toast Takeout

Toast's pricing includes free hardware with software subscription. Toast Access ($0/month) provides core POS features. Toast Essentials ($75/month) adds reporting and loyalty. Toast Custom ($165/month) includes advanced features and custom workflows.

Revel Systems: Enterprise Restaurant POS

Revel Systems provides enterprise restaurant POS for large chains and franchise organizations. The platform handles complex multi-location operations with centralized menu management, franchise permissions, and chain-wide reporting. Revel's iOS-based system provides reliability for high-volume restaurant operations.

Enterprise FeatureRevel CapabilityChain Operations
Central Menu ManagementChain-wide updatesMenu consistency
Franchise PermissionsLocation autonomyFranchise flexibility
Chain ReportingMulti-location analyticsPerformance benchmarking
Kitchen DisplayEnterprise KDSKitchen coordination
Delivery ManagementPlatform integrationDelivery operations
Guest ManagementCRM integrationCustomer tracking

Revel's enterprise features justify higher costs for chains requiring centralized control and franchise support. The platform scales to hundreds of locations with consistent operations.

TouchBistro: iPad Restaurant POS

TouchBistro provides iPad-based restaurant POS designed specifically for table service operations. The platform leverages iOS interface for intuitive server operation with tableside ordering capabilities. TouchBistro excels for restaurants preferring iPad hardware.

  • iPad-native restaurant POS interface
  • Tableside ordering with handheld iPad
  • Visual table mapping and status tracking
  • Menu management with course timing
  • Split checks and multiple payment types
  • Kitchen Display System integration
  • Staff scheduling and performance tracking
  • Reservation management integration

TouchBistro pricing starts at $69/month for basic features, increasing to $399/month for premium capabilities with advanced reporting and integrations.

Square for Restaurants: Budget Option

Square for Restaurants provides affordable POS for casual dining and quick-service restaurants. The platform offers basic restaurant features without premium depth. Square suits restaurants prioritizing low cost over advanced food service features.

Square restaurant features:

FeatureSquare for RestaurantsToast
Monthly Cost$0-60$0-165
Kitchen DisplayAdd-on ($50/month)Native
Table ManagementBasicExcellent
Menu BuilderBasic modifiersComplex modifiers
Course TimingNoYes
Delivery IntegrationAdd-onNative
Online OrderingAdd-onNative

Square's limitations include basic table management, no course timing, and add-on requirements for KDS and delivery. Restaurants with complex service needs should consider Toast instead.

Lightspeed Restaurant: Multi-Location Option

Lightspeed Restaurant provides restaurant POS capabilities integrated with Lightspeed's multi-location management. The platform handles table service with KDS support and menu management. Lightspeed suits restaurant groups operating multiple locations.

  • Table management with visual mapping
  • Kitchen Display System native support
  • Menu builder with modifier handling
  • Multi-location inventory management
  • Centralized reporting for restaurant groups
  • Staff management across locations
  • CRM integration for guest tracking

Lightspeed Restaurant requires Lightspeed Retail subscription ($69-199/month). The platform provides restaurant features without Toast's depth but excels for multi-location management.

POSitouch: Full-Service Restaurant Specialist

POSitouch provides specialized restaurant POS for full-service dining operations. The platform handles complex table service workflows including course timing, wine service, and fine dining operations. POSitouch suits upscale restaurants requiring sophisticated service management.

  • Advanced table management for fine dining
  • Course timing and pacing control
  • Wine service integration
  • Reservation system connection
  • Guest preference tracking
  • Split checks and payment flexibility
  • Kitchen Display with course routing

POSitouch pricing requires custom quote based on restaurant size and features. The platform suits establishments prioritizing service sophistication over cost.

NCR Aloha: Enterprise Legacy Platform

NCR Aloha provides established enterprise restaurant POS for large chains. The platform offers comprehensive features with long history in restaurant technology. Aloha handles complex chain operations with established reliability.

Enterprise CapabilityNCR AlohaRevel Systems
Platform AgeEstablished legacyModern cloud
Multi-LocationExcellentExcellent
Franchise SupportYesYes
Kitchen DisplayYesYes
Cloud ArchitectureHybridFull cloud
Update MethodManualAutomatic

NCR Aloha requires custom pricing and implementation. The platform suits large chains with legacy technology preferences.

Breadcrumb POS: Bar and Pub Focus

Breadcrumb POS (Upserve) specializes in bar and pub operations with features for beverage service. The platform handles high-volume drink orders, tab management, and bar-specific workflows.

  • Tab management and tracking
  • Quick drink ordering interface
  • Happy hour pricing automation
  • Bar inventory tracking
  • Tip pooling management
  • Kitchen Display for bar food
  • Guest tracking for regulars

Breadcrumb pricing starts at $99/month. The platform suits establishments prioritizing bar operations alongside food service.

Kitchen Display System Comparison

Kitchen Display System (KDS) coordination is critical for restaurant operations:

PlatformKDS TypeRouting FeaturesTiming DisplayCourse Management
ToastNative excellentStation routingYesCourse timing
RevelNative excellentMulti-stationYesCourse routing
TouchBistroNativeStation routingYesBasic
LightspeedNativeBasic routingYesNo
SquareAdd-onBasicNoNo

Toast and Revel provide the most sophisticated KDS with station routing, course timing, and prep station coordination. KDS displays show order items, timing, and routing information to kitchen staff.

Table Management Features

Table management coordinates dining room operations:

  • Visual Layout: Floor plan display with table status
  • Table Status: Available, seated, ordering, eating, closing
  • Guest Tracking: Party size, time seated, server assignment
  • Course Timing: Multi-course service pacing
  • Table Combining: Large party seating management
  • Server Assignment: Zone-based server allocation

Toast, Revel, and TouchBistro provide excellent table management. Square offers basic table tracking without course timing or sophisticated status management.

Menu Builder and Modifier Handling

Menu management handles complex food customization:

Menu FeatureToastRevelSquare
Modifier TypesUnlimited levelsComplex modifiersBasic modifiers
Modifier PricingPer-item pricingFlexible pricingAdd/subtract
Menu SectionsCategories/timesCategoriesCategories
Happy HourTime-based pricingTime-basedManual
Menu UpdatesReal-timeReal-timeReal-time
Recipe DisplayYesYesNo

Toast excels at modifier handling with unlimited customization levels, pricing flexibility, and recipe information display for kitchen staff.

Delivery Platform Integration

Delivery integration connects restaurants with delivery platforms:

  • Toast: Native DoorDash, UberEats, Grubhub integration with unified orders
  • Revel: Integration through delivery platform connectors
  • TouchBistro: Integration with major delivery platforms
  • Square: Add-on app required for delivery platform connection
  • Lightspeed: Integration through third-party connectors

Toast provides the best delivery integration with orders appearing directly in POS alongside in-house orders. Other platforms require connector apps with potential sync delays.

Payment Processing Features

Restaurant payment processing handles complex transactions:

Payment FeatureToastSquareTouchBistro
Split ChecksMultiple waysYesYes
Tip ProcessingAutomaticYesYes
Tip PoolingYesAdd-onYes
Gift CardsNativeNativeIntegration
House AccountsYesNoYes
Transaction Fee2.49%+15¢2.6%+10¢Varies

Split checks enable multiple payment methods per table. Tip processing calculates and distributes tips automatically. Toast provides the most flexible payment options for restaurants.

Online Ordering Integration

Online ordering connects digital customers to restaurant operations:

  • Toast Takeout: Native online ordering with POS integration
  • Square Online: Basic online ordering with Square integration
  • Revel: Custom online ordering integration
  • TouchBistro: Online ordering through integrations

Toast Takeout provides native online ordering integrated with Toast POS. Orders appear in Toast system alongside in-house orders for unified kitchen workflow.

Restaurant Analytics and Reporting

Restaurant-specific analytics provide operational insights:

Analytics CategoryToastRevelSquare
Menu EngineeringYesYesBasic
Server PerformanceYesYesYes
Table TurnoverYesYesNo
Daypart AnalysisYesYesYes
Kitchen TimingYesYesNo
Guest FrequencyYesYesBasic

Menu engineering analyzes item popularity versus profitability for menu optimization. Toast and Revel provide comprehensive restaurant analytics. Square offers basic reporting.

Hardware Requirements

Restaurant POS hardware includes specialized components:

Hardware ComponentToastTouchBistroSquare
POS TerminalToast hardwareiPadiPad/tablet
Kitchen DisplayToast KDSiPad KDSAdd-on
HandheldToast GoiPadSquare Terminal
Receipt PrinterIncludedAdd-onAdd-on
Card ReaderBuilt-inAdd-onBuilt-in

Toast provides integrated hardware with POS terminals, KDS screens, and handheld devices. TouchBistro operates on iPad hardware. Square uses tablets with add-on KDS.

Pricing Comparison

Restaurant POS pricing varies significantly:

PlatformEntry CostMid-tier CostEnterprise CostHardware
Toast$0/month$75/month$165/month$0-300
Square$0/month$60/monthN/A$150-300
TouchBistro$69/month$199/month$399/month$300-600
Revel$99/month$149/month$199/month$500-1,000
Lightspeed$69/month$119/month$199/month$300-600

Recommendations by Restaurant Type

Select restaurant POS based on operation type:

  • All Restaurant Types: Toast - comprehensive features, industry-specific
  • Enterprise Chains: Revel Systems - franchise support, centralized management
  • iPad Preference: TouchBistro - iOS-native, tableside ordering
  • Casual/Quick Service: Square for Restaurants - low cost, basic features
  • Multi-Location Groups: Lightspeed Restaurant - multi-store management
  • Fine Dining: POSitouch - course timing, wine service
  • Bars/Pubs: Breadcrumb - beverage focus, tab management
  • Large Legacy Chains: NCR Aloha - established enterprise platform
author: POS Systems Specialist

pros: Restaurant-specific features handle complex food service operations
Kitchen Display System integration coordinates kitchen workflow
Table management optimizes dining room operations and server efficiency
Menu builder supports complex modifiers for food customization
Delivery platform integration expands restaurant reach through online channels
cons: Restaurant POS systems cost more than general retail POS platforms
Specialized hardware requirements increase initial investment
Complex features require staff training for effective operation
Kitchen Display System adds additional hardware and maintenance
Some platforms like Square lack depth for full-service dining operations

faq.question: What is the best POS system for restaurants in 2026?
faq.answer: Toast remains the best restaurant POS system in 2026, providing comprehensive food service features including native Kitchen Display System, sophisticated table management, complex menu builder with unlimited modifiers, course timing for multi-course service, and delivery platform integration. Toast handles all restaurant types from quick-service to fine dining. Toast offers free hardware with software subscription, making it accessible for new restaurants. Revel Systems provides excellent enterprise restaurant POS for large chains.

faq.question: How does Kitchen Display System work with restaurant POS?
faq.answer: Kitchen Display System (KDS) receives orders directly from POS terminals, displaying items on screens in kitchen areas. Toast and Revel KDS route orders to appropriate prep stations (grill, cold station, fryer) based on item type. KDS shows order timing, prioritization, and completion status. Kitchen staff tap screens to mark items started and completed. Toast's course timing feature manages multi-course pacing, sending courses to KDS at appropriate intervals. KDS eliminates paper tickets, improves accuracy, and accelerates kitchen coordination.

faq.question: Can restaurant POS handle split checks and tip pooling?
faq.answer: Yes, major restaurant POS systems handle split checks and tip pooling. Toast, Revel, TouchBistro, and Lightspeed Restaurant support multiple split check methods including per-item splits, equal splits, and custom amounts. Tip processing calculates tips automatically on split payments. Tip pooling distributes tips among service staff based on hours worked or custom rules. Toast provides the most flexible tip pooling configurations. Square supports split checks but tip pooling requires add-on apps.
